what_it_does: Defines a systemd service unit that launches a Whisper.cpp speech-recognition server by running a local start script, sets the service PATH, logs output to the journal, and restarts on failure.
observations: Unit metadata includes version v1.2.0 and a last-updated date of 2025-12-01
Service type is simple with a single ExecStart pointing to /home/tim/.voicemode/services/whisper/bin/start-whisper-server.sh
The start script is expected to source voicemode.env for port/model configuration
Restart policy is on-failure with a 10-second delay
Standard output and error are sent to the systemd journal with the syslog identifier voicemode-whisper
standouts: PATH explicitly includes /usr/local/cuda/bin, suggesting GPU/CUDA usage
All runtime configuration is delegated to an external start script and env file
conventions: Follows standard systemd unit section ordering: Unit, Service, Install
Uses %h expansion for the user's home directory in PATH
Uses comments to record version and update metadata
Breaks portability by hardcoding /home/tim in ExecStart while using %h elsewhere
concerns: Hardcoded /home/tim path makes the unit non-portable and assumes a specific user
No User= or WorkingDirectory= directive is visible
Configuration behavior depends on files not shown in this unit
Restart=on-failure could hide persistent startup failures
No resource limits, timeout, or stop command are defined
notes: A thin systemd wrapper around a local Whisper.cpp server start script, with CUDA-aware PATH and journal logging.
questions: What exactly does start-whisper-server.sh do and where is voicemode.env located?
Is this intended as a user service or a system service?
Which Whisper model and port are selected by voicemode.env?
Is CUDA required, or only optional?
How are failures detected and what exit codes trigger a restart?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of running a Whisper.cpp speech recognition server matches the ExecStart command; the actual runtime configuration is intentionally delegated to the start script and env file.
intention_signals: It appears designed to keep the systemd unit minimal and stable while allowing model/port behavior to be controlled outside the unit via an environment file and start script.
novelty: The unit deliberately contains almost no Whisper-specific configuration, pushing all port/model decisions into a separately maintained start script and env file.
contribution: Provides a reusable systemd service template for launching a Whisper.cpp server, including restart-on-failure semantics, a CUDA-inclusive PATH, journal logging identity, and a convention of delegating configuration to an external start script.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd service unit that starts a local Whisper.cpp speech recognition server via a shell script, sets a CUDA-aware PATH, logs to journald, and restarts on failure.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a simple, resilient systemd-managed entry point for a user-local Whisper.cpp server whose model and port settings live outside the unit file.
